questions.question: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelt word is: d) WASSE WASTE: to use or spend something in a careless way or for something that is not necessary. FITTER: A person or thing that fits or is suited; a mechanic who fits or assembles parts. COURIER: A person or company that delivers messages, packages, and mail. COURSE: A direction or route taken or to be taken; a series of classes or a plan of study on a particular subject.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelt word is: a) PSEUDONYM Pseudonym: A fictitious name used by an author instead of their real name. Temporary: Lasting for only a limited period of time; not permanent. Taboo: A social or cultural prohibition or restriction. Camouflage: The use of any combination of materials, coloration, or illumination for concealment.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Save your breath
options: To make an effort that will be futile
questions.explanation: The most appropriate meaning of the given idiom "Save your breath" is: a) To make an effort that will be futile The idiom "save your breath" means to avoid wasting time or effort talking to someone, because it is clear that what you say will not have any effect or be futile.

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence are given as options. Identify the part that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error'. That will truly / been a milestone to / celebrate.
questions.explanation: The part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error is: b) been a milestone to The correct structure should be "That will truly be a milestone to celebrate." The verb "been" is incorrectly used in this context. The correct form is "be" to match the future tense indicated by "will." Therefore, the corrected answer would be: “That will truly be a milestone to celebrate.”

questions.question: Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words. A person who has given up their beliefs
questions.explanation: The word that can substitute the given group of words "A person who has given up their beliefs" is: c) Apostate • Apostate: A person who renounces a religious or political belief or principle. • Versatile: Able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions or activities. • Misanthrope: A person who dislikes humankind and avoids human society. • Crusade: A vigorous campaign for political, social, or religious change; also refers historically to the medieval military expeditions.

questions.question: Choose the word that means the same as the given word. Faddish
questions.explanation: The word that means the same as "Faddish" is: d) Fashionable • Faddish: Following a fashion that is popular for a short time; trendy. • Fashionable: Characteristic of, influenced by, or representing a current popular style. • Phony: Not genuine; fraudulent. • Unpopular: Not liked or popular. • Barge: A flat-bottomed boat for carrying freight, typically on canals and rivers.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. Participants were ______ a meal of steak and French fries under dim light.
questions.explanation: The appropriate option to fill in the blank is: c) served • Served: Means to provide food or drink to someone. • Nerved: Means to give courage or strength to someone. • Cured: Means to preserve food, especially meat, by salting, drying, or smoking. • Calmed: Means to make someone or something quiet or less agitated.

questions.question: Select the word that is closest in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below. DEPRESSED
questions.explanation: The word that is closest in meaning (synonym) to "DEPRESSED" is: b) DOLEFUL • Depressed: Feeling sad, dejected, or despondent. • Doleful: Expressing sorrow; mournful. • Sturdy: Strongly and solidly built; robust. • Noisy: Making a lot of noise; loud. • Dynamic: Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress.

questions.question: Choose the option that is the correct direct form of the sentence. She said, since it was evening, she had to go for a run.
options: She said, “It is evening I must ran.”
She said, “It is evening I must go for a run.”
She said, “It is evening I should go for a run.”
She said, “It is evening I might go for a run.”
questions.explanation: The correct answer is b: She said, “It is evening I must go for a run.” Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Modals will be changed according to the meaning of the sentence. Therefore, the correct answer is: She said, “It is evening I must go for a run.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate 'one word' for the expression given below. A person who can speak multiple languages.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate one-word term for a person who can speak multiple languages is: d) Polyglot Polyglot: A person who knows and is able to use several languages. Minaret: A tall slender tower, typically part of a mosque, with a balcony from which a muezzin calls Muslims to prayer. Spire: A tapering conical or pyramidal structure on the top of a building, typically a church tower. Intestate: Not having made a will before one dies.

questions.question: The given sentence is in active voice. Change the voice of the sentence. Select the correct option from the sentences given in options. Vasant has delivered the speech.
options: The speech was timely delivered by Vasant.
The speech was delivered by Vasant.
Vasant delivers the speech on time.
The speech has been delivered by Vasant.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is d: The speech has been delivered by Vasant. Active voice ---- Passive voice Subject + has/have + V3 + Object ---- Object + has/have + been + V3 + by + Subject Therefore, the correct passive voice is: “The speech has been delivered by Vasant.”

questions.question: Select the word that is closest in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below. THRIFTY
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: PRUDENT "THRIFTY," which means economical or careful with money. Prudent: Showing care and thought for the future; wise or judicious in practical affairs, especially regarding money. Extravagant: Spending money or resources freely and recklessly; wasteful. Generous: Showing a readiness to give more of something, especially money, than is strictly necessary or expected. Wasteful: Using or expending resources carelessly, extravagantly, or to no purpose.

questions.question: Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Abduct
questions.explanation: The word that is opposite in meaning to "Abduct" is: c) Redeem • Abduct: To take (someone) away illegally by force or deception; kidnap. • Hijack: To seize (a vehicle, especially an airplane) by forceful or illegal means in order to commandeer it. • Sociable: Willing to talk and engage in activities with other people; friendly. • Redeem: To compensate for the faults or bad aspects of (something); to rescue or save. • Impress: to make somebody feel admiration and respect

questions.question: Some parts of one or more sentences have been jumbled up, and labelled A, B, C and D. Select the option that gives the correct sequence in which these parts can be rearranged to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence. Their approach sensitized A. of it. Similarly, behind them, in the halls, B. flicked on when they came within ten feet C. a switch somewhere and the nursery light D. lights went on and off as they left them behind, with a soft automaticity.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is d: CBAD The correct sequence to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence is: C. a switch somewhere and the nursery light B. flicked on when they came within ten feet A. of it. Similarly, behind them, in the halls, D. lights went on and off as they left them behind, with a soft automaticity. Therefore, the correct sequence is: CBAD.

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence are given as options. Identify the part that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select 'No error'. Shyam, so good with weapons, fail to find the words / to tell him that everything he’d done / so far, all the compromises he’d made, had been for love.
options: Shyam, so good with weapons, fail to find the words
to tell him that everything he’d done
so far, all the compromises he’d made, had been for love.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is b: Shyam, so good with weapons, fail to find the words The subject "Shyam" does not match with the verb "fail" in number. The correct form of the verb should be "fails" to agree with the singular subject "Shyam." Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: “Shyam, so good with weapons, fails to find the words.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. In the early autumn fierce fighting took place, the __________ Serbians bearing a prominent part.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is: b) gallant "Gallant" means brave, heroic, or chivalrous, which fits the context of fierce fighting. Cowardly: Showing a lack of courage or bravery; timid or fearful. Unabashed: Not embarrassed, disconcerted, or ashamed. "Bear," which means to carry or support; in this context, it would mean that the Serbians played a role or participated actively.

questions.question: Some parts of a few sentences have been jumbled up, and labelled A, B, C and D. Select the option that gives the correct sequence in which these parts can be rearranged to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence. My years in my father’s house A. would have been unbearable had B. the feel of his hand clutching mine, his C. I not had my brother. I never forgot D. refusal to abandon me. Perhaps he and I would have been close even otherwise.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: ACBD The correct sequence to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence is: A. would have been unbearable had C. I not had my brother. I never forgot B. the feel of his hand clutching mine, his D. refusal to abandon me. Perhaps he and I would have been close even otherwise. Therefore, the correct sequence is: ACBD.

questions.question: Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words. One who does not express oneself freely
questions.explanation: The word that can substitute the given group of words "One who does not express oneself freely" is: d) Introvert • Introvert is someone who tends to be more reserved, introspective, and less inclined to speak freely in social situations. • Pessimist is someone who tends to see the negative side of things. • Erudite person is someone who has deep, extensive knowledge on various subjects. • Skeptic is someone who doubts or questions the validity or truth of something.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. ______ threats to elephants requires public and political will to take action.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: Addressing Addressing: Dealing with or confronting a problem or issue directly. Perplexing: Something that is confusing or difficult to understand. Dignifying: Enhancing or elevating the status or worth of something, often implying respect or honor. Stagnating: Remaining unchanged or making little or no progress, often implying a lack of development or growth.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Burn your bridges
options: To eliminate the possibility of return or retreat
questions.explanation: The correct answer is d: To eliminate the possibility of return or retreat The idiom "burn your bridges" means to take actions that make it impossible to go back to a previous situation or relationship.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The man with the shotgun (1) ________ the guard to his knees. He handed up the shotgun to his partner and yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked (2) ________ together with a pair of handcuffs. He (3) ________ him onto the floor with a kick between the shoulder blades. Then he took his shotgun back and (4) ________ to the security gate at the end of the counter. He was short and heavy and moved with peculiar slowness. “Buzz him (5) _______,” his partner said. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 1.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option for blank No. 1, "pushed" would be the best fit. Pushed: To apply force to move someone or something away from oneself or from a particular place. Steered: To guide or direct the movement of someone or something in a particular direction, often using physical force or manipulation. Motivated: To provide a reason or incentive for someone to act in a certain way or to achieve a particular goal. Swayed: To cause someone or something to move gently back and forth or from side to side, often as a result of physical force or influence.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The man with the shotgun (1) ________ the guard to his knees. He handed up the shotgun to his partner and yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked (2) ________ together with a pair of handcuffs. He (3) ________ him onto the floor with a kick between the shoulder blades. Then he took his shotgun back and (4) ________ to the security gate at the end of the counter. He was short and heavy and moved with peculiar slowness. “Buzz him (5) _______,” his partner said. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 2.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option for blank No. 2, "them" would be the best fit. "Them" is a pronoun used to refer to a group of people or things. In this context, "them" refers to the guard's wrists. The sentence means that the man with the shotgun yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked those wrists together with a pair of handcuffs.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The man with the shotgun (1) ________ the guard to his knees. He handed up the shotgun to his partner and yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked (2) ________ together with a pair of handcuffs. He (3) ________ him onto the floor with a kick between the shoulder blades. Then he took his shotgun back and (4) ________ to the security gate at the end of the counter. He was short and heavy and moved with peculiar slowness. “Buzz him (5) _______,” his partner said. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 3.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option for blank No. 3, "toppled" would be the best fit. Toppled: To cause something to fall over, typically because of a sudden movement or force. Straightened: To make something become straight or upright. Placed: To put something in a particular position or location. Ascended: To move upward or rise to a higher position or level.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The man with the shotgun (1) ________ the guard to his knees. He handed up the shotgun to his partner and yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked (2) ________ together with a pair of handcuffs. He (3) ________ him onto the floor with a kick between the shoulder blades. Then he took his shotgun back and (4) ________ to the security gate at the end of the counter. He was short and heavy and moved with peculiar slowness. “Buzz him (5) _______,” his partner said. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 4.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option for blank No. 4, "went over" Went over: This phrase means to move toward or approach something. In this context, it suggests that the man moved toward the security gate at the end of the counter, which makes sense given the situation described in the passage.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The man with the shotgun (1) ________ the guard to his knees. He handed up the shotgun to his partner and yanked the guard's wrists up behind his back and locked (2) ________ together with a pair of handcuffs. He (3) ________ him onto the floor with a kick between the shoulder blades. Then he took his shotgun back and (4) ________ to the security gate at the end of the counter. He was short and heavy and moved with peculiar slowness. “Buzz him (5) _______,” his partner said. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank No. 5.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option for blank No. 5, "in" "In" is the most appropriate option in this context. It implies that the partner wants the man with the shotgun to allow or permit the guard to enter a particular area or location, likely through the security gate at the end of the counter.
